novel_features.name: Ready-to-drive composite
novel_features.description: Single yes/no answer to "can I leave in 5 minutes?" with reasoned blocker list - SOC vs trip distance, plugged-in, doors closed, sentry off, cabin within 3F of target, no mid-install update
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local join across the last vehicle_states snapshot and synced metadata; the Tesla API does not expose a composite readiness endpoint
novel_features.why_it_matters: Most-asked Tesla owner question; the iOS app makes it a 6-tap diagnostic, agents need a one-call truth

novel_features.name: Charging cost ledger
novel_features.description: Per-session cost, monthly spend, home-vs-Supercharger ratio with tariff-window aware $/kWh
novel_features.rationale: Joins local charges + tariffs tables; the Tesla API returns raw kWh but not stitched cost by tariff window. TeslaMate proves demand but requires Postgres+Grafana
novel_features.why_it_matters: Charging cost is the #1 Tesla-owner spreadsheet exercise; this is the first pure-CLI replacement for the TeslaMate dashboard
novel_features.group: Local analytics that beat the dashboard

novel_features.name: Cost counterfactual
novel_features.description: "If you had only charged at home you would have saved $X over the last 90 days." Re-runs charge rows with home $/kWh substituted for Supercharger sessions
novel_features.rationale: Only possible because the ledger lives locally; no API exposes the counterfactual
novel_features.why_it_matters: Most Tesla owners suspect they could save by charging at home but lack proof; this puts a dollar figure on it
novel_features.group: Local analytics that beat the dashboard

novel_features.name: Supercharger queue watch
novel_features.description: Single-poll snapshot of free stalls at a saved Supercharger; --watch repeats every N seconds with JSON-lines transitions
novel_features.rationale: Polls NEARBY_CHARGING_SITES stall-availability subfield. Tesla shows this in-car only; no third-party CLI/agent exposes it
novel_features.why_it_matters: Avoids the I-90 Issaquah queue at peak; pageable from an agent on a drive

novel_features.name: Drive-and-charge timeline
novel_features.description: Stitched drives + charges from synced vehicle_states polls - start/end, distance, energy, efficiency, address-resolved lat/lng
novel_features.rationale: Stitches consecutive vehicle_states rows where shift_state or charging_state transitions; reproduces TeslaMate's primary view without Postgres
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets agents reason over the same data TeslaMate exposes, without standing up Docker+Postgres+Grafana
novel_features.group: Local analytics that beat the dashboard

novel_features.name: Vampire-drain monitor
novel_features.description: SOC delta vs idle time, flags suspicious sentry sessions or app-wake events
novel_features.rationale: Reads vehicle_states rows where shift_state=null and charging=disconnected; computes %/24h drain trend
novel_features.why_it_matters: Warranty-dispute evidence and rogue-sentry detection; TeslaMate dashboard only otherwise
novel_features.group: Local analytics that beat the dashboard

novel_features.name: Keys audit
novel_features.description: Lists every enrolled key with last-seen, role, form-factor; flags keys not seen >90d as stale candidates for removal
novel_features.rationale: Joins keys_enrolled with commands_log for last-seen derivation; no Tesla UI exposes this
novel_features.why_it_matters: Security-minded owners worry about old phones and abandoned NFC cards still pairing with their car; this is the quarterly review

novel_features.name: Reachability doctor
novel_features.description: Detects signed-command-required errors, classifies the vehicle as REST-friendly vs signed-command-required, prints tesla-control enrollment URL when needed
novel_features.rationale: Pings vehicle_data + a benign command/honk; parses the error envelope; only us because no competitor explains the signed-command landmine to the user
novel_features.why_it_matters: Tesla's 2024-2026 signed-command rollout is the #1 user-facing landmine for any Tesla CLI; this is the only one that surfaces it clearly

narrative.headline: Every Tesla mobile-app feature, plus a charging-cost ledger and supercharger queue watcher no other Tesla CLI ships.
narrative.value_prop: The Tesla owner-API as JSON-first commands, with a local SQLite that remembers every vehicle state. Get a single `ready` yes/no for departure, a tariff-aware charging cost ledger that replaces TeslaMate's Docker stack, and a supercharger queue watcher you can subscribe to from an agent. For 2018+ Models S/X and pre-2021 Models 3/Y, commands route end-to-end on plain REST; newer vehicles get a clear shim message via `tesla reachability`.
narrative.auth_narrative: Tesla owner API uses an 8-hour bearer token issued by the iOS/Android app's OAuth flow. The CLI ships `tesla auth login --paste` for one-time refresh-token capture; the refresh token lives in your macOS Keychain (or `~/.config/tesla-pp-cli/auth.json` mode 600 elsewhere) and silently re-mints the bearer on 401. No Tesla partner registration required for personal use; the Fleet API (signed commands) is documented in `tesla doctor` for newer-vehicle owners.

quickstart.comment: After you set TESLA_AUTH_TOKEN (see Auth section), this confirms auth and lists your vehicle_id / VINs / energy sites
Confirm auth and discover your vehicle_id / VINs / energy site IDs
Confirm your vehicle responds to REST commands (vs needing signed-command enrollment)
Capture a vehicle_data snapshot for every car on your account; populates the local store for analytics
Single yes/no "can I leave?" with the blocker list
Last 30 days of charging cost, home vs Supercharger split

troubleshoots.fix: Your vehicle is on Tesla's new signed-command protocol. Run `tesla doctor` for enrollment URL; install `tesla-control` from teslamotors/vehicle-command for command operations
Run `tesla auth refresh`; if that fails, capture a fresh refresh token via tesla_auth and `tesla auth login --paste`
Run snapshots on a cron with `tesla snap --all` and avoid keeping the car awake unnecessarily; check `tesla doctor` for asleep-state behavior and battery drain mitigation

troubleshoots.symptom: `tesla cost ledger` shows $0 for Supercharger sessions
troubleshoots.fix: Run `tesla snap --all` to refresh vehicle_data, then `tesla timeline` to stitch new charge sessions into `tesla_charges`; Supercharger pricing may also lag in the upstream CHARGING_HISTORY by up to 24h

narrative.when_to_use: Use tesla-pp-cli when you want a JSON-first CLI for your Tesla owner account: vehicle state inspection, climate/lock/charge commands, charging cost analytics, and an MCP surface for AI agents to drive your car. Faster than the iOS app for one-off commands, more agent-friendly than TeslaPy, and ships a local SQLite that delivers TeslaMate-style analytics (drives, charges, vampire drain, cost ledger) without standing up Postgres+Grafana. Best fit for owners with pre-2021 Models S/X or 3/Y on the old REST endpoints; newer vehicles get a clear shim via `tesla reachability`.

recipes.title: Morning ready check
recipes.explanation: One JSON object: is the car ready to drive, and if not, why not. Use `--select` to limit response size for agents.

recipes.title: Monthly charging-cost report
recipes.explanation: Per-month breakdown with dotted-path `--select` so the agent sees only the rollup fields, not every session.

recipes.title: Preconditioning before leaving
recipes.explanation: Start climate preconditioning. Run `tesla ready <vin>` ten minutes later to confirm cabin warmed up.

recipes.title: Supercharger queue watcher
recipes.explanation: Subscribe to free-stall transitions at a saved site; emits one JSON line per transition. Pipe to an agent for alerting.

recipes.title: Local SOC history via analytics
recipes.explanation: Pull the 30 most recent vehicle-state rows from the local SQLite for trend analysis; same data TeslaMate would surface in Grafana.

trigger_phrases: how much did i spend on charging this month
send this address to my tesla
